source_old_req: The GCAS_Monitor component shall define vertical acceleration as 100.0, expected impact time as 15.0, and recovery time as 5.0. The GCAS_Monitor_impl implementation shall calculate the altitudeFromGround variable by invoking the Agree_Nodes::height function using the values of the altitude input port, the verticalVelocity input port, and a minimum height time of 0.5 seconds. Furthermore, the implementation shall guarantee that when the altitude input port value is less than or equal to 30.0 or the calculated altitudeFromGround value is less than or equal to 500.0, the gcasRequested output signal must be true.

expected_check.note: Existing requirement kept because it is explicit and non-vague.

quality_policy.traceable: Every item has trace_refs and expected_check metadata tied to AADL facts.
quality_policy.principle: Every generated sentence must name an explicit property assignment or structural connection; ordinary feature declarations are not used as rebuilt requirements.

behavior_style_policy.basis: front-110 AGREE-derived requirements favor implementable behavior, constants, assumptions, guarantees, routing, and bounded properties.
behavior_style_policy.rule: Do not describe unsupported validity/legal-boundary semantics unless the AADL model exposes variables, values, or properties that can implement them.

natural_requirement_before_paragraph_rew: The GCAS_Monitor component shall define vertical acceleration as 100.0, expected impact time as 15.0, and recovery time as 5.0. The GCAS_Monitor_impl implementation shall calculate the altitudeFromGround variable by invoking the Agree_Nodes::height function using the values of the altitude input port, the verticalVelocity input port, and a minimum height time of 0.5 seconds. paragraph_style_policy.rule: Req.txt is written as a natural-language requirement paragraph; per-item traceability stays in requirement_items.
paragraph_style_policy.basis: Front-110 AGREE-derived requirements generally combine function, assumptions, guarantees, and implementation notes into 1-4 sentence paragraphs.
